name: Sam Rivers
abstract: Samuel Carthorne Rivers (September 25, 1923 – December 26, 2011) was an American jazz musician and composer. Though most famously a tenor saxophonist, he also performed on soprano saxophone, bass clarinet, flute, harmonica, piano and viola.

about_quotes.speaker: Longineu Parsons
about_quotes.evidence: He even got to play with Sam Rivers and he got to play with all kinds of people
about_quotes.doc_title: Longineu Parsons interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 17, 2026

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: I don’t know about Sam Rivers. I don’t recall knowing Sam Rivers as a student.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joseph Daley
about_quotes.evidence: The whole thing is what happens with a lot of musicians when they play... It’s the same thing with the improvisation that we’re talking about with Sam. When we start we have no idea where it’s going to end... He starts off and structures us and guides us through
about_quotes.doc_title: Joseph Daley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 18, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Galper
about_quotes.evidence: I apprentice with Sam for six years. He was my post-graduate work. ... But I learned so much from Sam anyway. I mean he taught me a lot.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Galper interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 27, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Stewart
about_quotes.evidence: I was playing free jazz in Sam Rivers’ Big Band that was performing and working and rehearsing at this time all through the seventies
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Stewart interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 24, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: David Friesen
about_quotes.evidence: So I play concerts with Sam Rivers and I’ve played, I was part of the free music scene in Seattle in the ‘60s.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Friesen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 24, 2024
